seoTitle: Create a Partial Invoice in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Set up progress billing in QuickBooks Online to invoice customers for project milestones from a master estimate, step by step.
intro: Billing a large project in stages, rather than all at once, needs the right setup. This guide covers how to turn on progress invoicing in QuickBooks Online and convert a master estimate into partial invoices tied to project milestones.

steps.heading: Enable progress invoicing
steps.body: Go to the gear icon, select Account and Settings, open the Sales tab, and toggle on Progress Invoicing.

steps.heading: Draft a master estimate
steps.body: Create a new Estimate covering the full project scope and save it as your baseline document.

steps.heading: Create an invoice from the estimate
steps.body: Open the saved estimate and click Create invoice to start a partial billing run.

steps.heading: Choose your billing parameters
steps.body: In the progress billing pop-up, select whether to bill a percentage, a fixed amount, or specific line items for this milestone.

steps.heading: Review and send
steps.body: Check that the partial totals match your intended milestone, add a note describing the phase covered, then save and send the invoice.

faq.question: Do I need an estimate before I can create a partial invoice?
faq.answer: Yes, progress invoicing works by converting portions of an existing estimate into individual invoices.

faq.question: Can I bill a flat percentage instead of specific line items?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks lets you choose a percentage of the remaining balance, a fixed dollar amount, or specific items for each partial invoice.

faq.question: How do I track how much of the project has been billed?
faq.answer: Run the Progress Invoicing Summary by Customer report to see how much of each estimate has been invoiced so far.

faq.question: Will the partial invoice show the original project total?
faq.answer: Yes, partial invoices typically display the original estimate total, current billing amount, and remaining balance for context.
